name: Sprint 0 — Analysis & Architecture
goal: Analyze the legacy MSSQL .bak database, design the target PostgreSQL schema, define all API contracts, and document the solution structure. Zero production code written. All output is documentation for human review before Sprint 1 begins.
approval_gate: HUMAN MUST APPROVE sprint-0-approval.json output artifacts before Sprint 1 starts

stories.title: [ARCHITECT] Analyze MSSQL .bak file and extract legacy schema
stories.description: Mount or restore the 4.4MB MSSQL backup. Extract all DDL. Document every table, column, FK, and index. Map legacy customers + contact history columns to new schema. Flag PII fields. Map MSSQL types to PostgreSQL equivalents.
acceptance_criteria: All legacy tables documented with estimated row counts
Column mapping: old → new defined for customers and contact history
MSSQL-specific types mapped to PostgreSQL equivalents

stories.title: [ARCHITECT] Design PostgreSQL target schema
stories.description: Design full PostgreSQL schema for: Projects, Users, UserProjectRoles, Customers, ContactHistory, Notes, Tasks, Opportunities, SyncLogs, RefreshTokens, AuditLogs. Every table has Id(UUID), CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, IsDeleted. Every tenant table has ProjectId. Define all enums, indexes, and FK relationships.
acceptance_criteria: All entities have base fields (Id, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, IsDeleted)
Indexes identified on ProjectId, Email, ExternalId
Schema validated against legacy migration mapping

stories.title: [ARCHITECT] Define API contracts and OpenAPI spec
stories.description: Define all REST endpoints: Auth, SuperAdmin, Customers, ContactHistory, Notes, Tasks, Opportunities, Sync, Dashboard. Specify request/response schemas, required roles, error codes. Define SaaS A and SaaS B inbound payload formats. Define outbound callback payload format.
acceptance_criteria: All endpoints listed with method, path, auth requirement, allowed roles
Sync payload formats for SaaS A, SaaS B, and outbound callbacks defined

stories.title: [ARCHITECT] Define solution folder structure and project scaffold plan
stories.description: Document the exact .NET solution folder structure (Domain, Application, Infrastructure, API, Tests), frontend folder structure, CI/CD workflow layout, and Docker Compose setup. List all required environment variables in .env.example template.
acceptance_criteria: Every project layer mapped to Clean Architecture purpose
DI registration strategy described per layer
Docker Compose services listed (postgres, mssql, api, frontend)
.env.example contains all required variable names with no real values

risks.risk: MSSQL .bak file may require full SQL Server license to restore
risks.mitigation: Use mssql Docker image (Developer Edition — free for dev use) or mssql-scripter CLI tool

risks.risk: Legacy schema may have unexpected tables or naming conventions
risks.mitigation: ION-001 does full discovery before any mapping decisions are made

risks.risk: SaaS A and SaaS B payload formats unknown until .bak analysis
risks.mitigation: ION-003 deferred until ION-001 complete; API contract placeholders used
